title: Inpainting replaces only a controlled region
canonical_summary: Inpainting is a targeted image edit where the model modifies a masked region while using surrounding context to preserve the rest of the image.
source_priority_rule: Canonical inpainting guidance outranks generic image prompting advice.
answer_pattern: Explain mask-driven localized editing and the role of surrounding context.
exactness_guard: Do not describe inpainting as full-image regeneration only.

title: Mask quality controls edit boundaries
canonical_summary: The mask defines the edit target, and poor mask edges can cause bleeding, halos, or unwanted changes outside the desired region.
source_priority_rule: Mask guidance outranks prompt-only explanations for boundary failures.
answer_pattern: Explain that mask shape, feathering, and context affect boundary quality.
exactness_guard: Do not assume every boundary issue is only a prompt issue.

title: Denoise controls how much the region changes
canonical_summary: Denoise strength controls how aggressively the masked region is rewritten. Too high can ignore the original; too low may not change enough.
source_priority_rule: Denoise theory outranks broad style advice.
answer_pattern: Explain denoise as the edit-strength control and recommend testing small changes.
exactness_guard: Do not promise one universal denoise value.

title: Context matters for coherent edits
canonical_summary: Inpainting depends on nearby image context. Cropping too tightly or masking without enough surrounding context can reduce coherence.
source_priority_rule: Workflow context guidance outranks prompt-only fixes.
answer_pattern: Explain context area and crop/stitch as coherence controls.
exactness_guard: Do not treat prompt wording as the only factor.

title: Inpaint changes the whole person or object
canonical_summary: If inpaint changes body shape or surrounding structure, the mask or conditioning is too broad, denoise is too high, or structural controls are missing.
source_priority_rule: Mask/denoise/structure evidence outranks creative prompt advice.
answer_pattern: Identify mask scope and structural preservation as the likely issue.
exactness_guard: Do not blame only the text prompt for shape drift.

title: Visible seam or halo at mask edge
canonical_summary: Seams and halos often come from hard mask edges, insufficient feathering, or mismatch between generated region and surrounding pixels.
answer_pattern: Recommend checking mask edge, feathering, and context.
exactness_guard: Do not diagnose a seam as a runtime failure.

title: Inpaint ignores requested change
canonical_summary: If an inpaint edit barely changes, denoise may be too low, prompt may be weak, or the model lacks enough freedom inside the mask.
source_priority_rule: Denoise and prompt guidance jointly drive diagnosis.
answer_pattern: Recommend increasing denoise gradually or clarifying the target prompt.
exactness_guard: Do not jump straight to changing models.

title: Mask problem vs denoise problem
canonical_summary: If the wrong area changes, inspect mask. If the right area changes too much or too little, inspect denoise.
answer_pattern: Branch by area selection vs edit strength.
exactness_guard: Do not change several variables at once.

title: Inpainting vs restoration/upscale
canonical_summary: Use inpainting for localized semantic edits; use restoration/upscale for broad detail recovery or resolution improvement.
answer_pattern: Separate localized replacement from global enhancement.
exactness_guard: Do not use inpainting as the answer for every image defect.

title: Trust mask/workflow evidence first
canonical_summary: For inpaint failures, actual mask, denoise value, crop/context, and workflow settings outrank generic prompt advice.
source_priority_rule: Visible mask/workflow evidence outranks broad prompt theory.
answer_pattern: Ask for or inspect mask/settings before recommending large changes.
exactness_guard: Do not invent settings without workflow evidence.

title: Do not confuse inpainting with try-on transfer
canonical_summary: Inpainting can edit clothing or regions, but true garment transfer/try-on needs stronger shape and garment-conditioning logic.
answer_pattern: Explain that inpainting may be part of try-on but is not the whole try-on system.
exactness_guard: Do not promise accurate garment transfer from simple inpainting.

title: How to answer inpainting questions
user_phrasing: how should assistant answer inpaint issues
canonical_summary: Answers should identify the target region, inspect mask/denoise/context, name likely failure class, and recommend one controlled adjustment.
answer_pattern: Use: target -> mask -> denoise -> context -> one test change.
exactness_guard: Do not recommend changing mask, denoise, model, prompt, and resolution all at once.
